title: #1 Rule For A Perfect First Date (Matthew Hussey, Get The Guy)
text: there is a fatal mistake that people make when they're planning a date I'm gonna tell you exactly what that mistake is and at the end of the video I'm gonna show you how to avoid this because this is this is the worst it creates every awkward date that you will ever go on now here's the mistake when people go on a date they create this big plan for what they're gonna do with someone that is almost impossible to escape from what would be an example of that dinner a dinner date is just an awful idea from for the most part unless you already know you like the person and they like you and you already have a connection and attraction going to a dinner date is really a nightmare because let's say 15 minutes in you realize that you and this person have nothing in common they just said something that that you thought was completely batshit crazy and you think I can't I cannot be in this person's company for the next hour except you just ordered your main course now you're stuck there for the next 45 minutes to an hour with this person and then at the end of the main course what do you do if he orders dessert you're screwed for another 30 minutes and it also looks kind of desperate when you don't know somebody when you don't know if you have attraction and you've booked out your whole evening to see them the message that we send is I have a whole evening for someone I barely even know how do we get around this I want you to think of your date as being three compartments and how you organize these three compartments will determine how easy it is to escape how stressful the day is and how in control and cool you look in the process going to dinner is like having 2/3 of the date up front that's why it's dangerous because now 20 minutes in you can't leave what I suggest is taking one compartment the first compartment and having that take place at a separate venue to begin with than the remaining two-thirds let's say you wanted to go for a drink in a bar that's 1/3 because it's easy to remove yourself from that situation if you want to leave after one drink because it's not going very well you you can make your excuses I need to catch up with my friends or you know I see my friends I have to go I need to go home

title: #1 Rule For A Perfect First Date (Matthew Hussey, Get The Guy)
text: s easy to remove yourself from that situation if you want to leave after one drink because it's not going very well you you can make your excuses I need to catch up with my friends or you know I see my friends I have to go I need to go home and get some work done whatever it is use that one third as the first venue that you go to now you can save the remaining two compartments for something else let's say there's a bowling alley around the corner and you don't tell him about that to begin with but the first drink goes really well and after a drink or two you say to him you know what let's go do something fun there's a bowling alley around the corner are you are you good I'm gonna kick your ass let's go bowling having one compartment take place somewhere that's easy and risk-free serves two big purposes the first is having an escape plan if it's not going well you can leave and you haven't committed yourself to the next two hours of something with someone that you don't really like but the second thing and this is more subtle it shows that if he's graduating to the second two compartments of the day it's because he's done something right he's earned it if you had a great drink and then you said you know there's a bowling alley around the corner let's go bowling he's going bowling with you because he feels like you've had a good time and now spontaneously you're saying let's go do something else in your mind you already had an idea that you might be able to do something else with him by the way that planning is key right you don't want to be stuck having a drink in a bar and have no ideas of where to take this day if it's going well but it's showing him that this is arising from the fact that he's done well and he's earned it you haven't just given up an entire evening to someone who you don't know just because you're doing it because you like him the point is divide it like this so that you don't put all of the pressure around one activity in a day the ideal date does one compartment somewhere that's risk free and easy to leave and then if it's going well you have the second two-thirds in the next place that's it as I said see your date has three compartments it's going to be much easier to manage and I'll see you next week for the next tip I'm excited I hope you're enjoying these leave me a comment if you're not following me on Facebook right now go to facebook.com for would slash coach Matthew Hussey I have articles there videos you
